entries.summary: Google announced Bard, an experimental conversational AI service, in response to ChatGPT.
Google opened public access to Bard for users in the United States and Britain.
At Google I/O, the company expanded Bard and previewed PaLM 2 model.
Meta released Llama 2 free for commercial use, partnering with Microsoft Azure.
Seven leading AI firms made voluntary safety commitments at the White House.
Amazon announced an investment of up to four billion dollars in Anthropic.
World leaders attended Britain's first AI Safety Summit at Bletchley Park.
Google launched Gemini, a multimodal model it positioned against OpenAI's GPT-4.
Google rebranded Bard as Gemini and introduced a paid Gemini Advanced tier.
Google paused Gemini's image generation after historical figure inaccuracies emerged.
Anthropic released Claude 3, claiming benchmark gains over GPT-4 on reasoning.
Meta released Llama 3, calling it the most capable openly available large language model.
OpenAI showed GPT-4o, prompting Google to accelerate its own real-time voice demos.
Google unveiled Project Astra, a prototype universal AI agent, at its I/O event.
Apple announced Apple Intelligence at WWDC, partnering with OpenAI for ChatGPT access.
Meta released Llama 3.1 with a 405-billion-parameter open-weight model variant.
Anthropic launched Claude Enterprise, expanding into the corporate productivity market.
Anthropic introduced computer use, letting Claude operate desktops on a user's behalf.
Google introduced Gemini 2.0 with built-in agentic capabilities for developers.
Google announced Bard, its rival to ChatGPT, ahead of an AI-focused event.
Google's Bard demo error wiped roughly 100 billion dollars off Alphabet's market value.
Google opened a waiting list for public access to its Bard chatbot.
Meta launched a free Llama 2 model alongside an expanded Microsoft partnership.
Major AI firms pledged at the White House to mark and test AI-generated content.
Amazon said it would invest up to four billion dollars in AI startup Anthropic.
Britain hosted the world's first AI Safety Summit at Bletchley Park.
Google introduced Gemini, claiming the model outperformed GPT-4 on key benchmarks.
Google renamed its Bard chatbot Gemini and offered a paid Advanced subscription.
Google paused Gemini image generation after it produced historically inaccurate depictions.
Anthropic announced Claude 3, saying it outperformed rivals on common reasoning tests.
Meta released Llama 3, free for commercial use and offered through Microsoft Azure.
Google previewed Astra, an AI assistant prototype, at its annual I/O developer event.
Apple unveiled Apple Intelligence at WWDC, with OpenAI helping power some features.
Meta released Llama 3.1, including a flagship 405-billion-parameter open-weight variant.
Anthropic released a Claude version able to take control of a computer.
Google released Gemini 2.0, marketed as its most capable model to date.
